exemplar.title: How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief and opinion?

detailedExemplarData.title: How can we distinguish between knowledge, belief and opinion?

strengths: Clearly identifies three specific real-world objects with contextual detail
Makes sustained, mostly clear links between each object and the chosen TOK prompt
Supports several points with cited sources, indicating engagement with external research
weaknesses: Analysis is uneven in depth, relying at times on broad assertions rather than nuanced exploration or counterclaims
Definitions of key terms are simplistic and sometimes repetitive, reducing conceptual precision
Citation formatting is inconsistent and some references lack full details, undermining academic rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition fulfills the descriptors for the 5â6 band: three well-contextualized objects are linked clearly to the chosen prompt, and justifications draw on TOK concepts with some external support. However, the uneven depth of analysis, simplistic definitions, occasional repetition, and inconsistent citation practice prevent it from reaching the 7â8 band. A final score of 6 reflects the upper-end performance within the 5â6 band.

annotations.comment: Footnote formatting is inconsistent and the source lacks author details; adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., MLA or APA) for academic rigor.
The screenshot is specific, but an analysis of the mediumâs influence on opinion formation (e.g., echo chambers) would deepen the TOK connection.
The description of the tweet context is informative but misses explicit reference to how social media platforms mediate knowledge and opinion.
This paragraph defining opinion is overly broad; incorporating TOK concepts like personal and shared knowledge or biases would strengthen theoretical grounding.
The remark on expert views shaping opinions raises an interesting point but is unsupported by examples; integrate specific instances to substantiate the claim.
The discussion on how opinions change due to events is insightful; however, linking this dynamic to Ways of Knowing (e.g., sense perception, emotion) would align with TOK frameworks.
Identifying the evil eye bracelet as a personal, culturally significant object demonstrates clear real-world context, fulfilling the requirement for specific artefacts rather than generic types.
The image description is detailed, but the student could integrate observations from the artefactâs physical attributes directly into the TOK analysis to deepen the epistemological link.

The contextual background of the bracelet is clear, but the student should connect the gifting ritual more directly to TOK concepts such as shared knowledge frameworks or communal knowledge production.

The definition of belief offered is overly descriptive and lacks engagement with established TOK definitions (e.g., justified true belief). Incorporating academic definitions would enhance conceptual precision.
The concluding sentence is cut off mid-idea and does not fully articulate how the bracelet exemplifies belief distinct from opinion and knowledge; ensure completeness for clarity.
This transitional sentence repeats earlier points without adding new insight; consolidate overlapping ideas to maintain focus and avoid redundancy.
Selecting a class 10 science textbook provides a tangible example of knowledge production, aligning well with the promptâs distinction between belief and evidence-based understanding.
While the cover image anchors the object in reality, referencing a specific chapter or experiment from the textbook would more effectively illustrate how knowledge claims are justified.
The explanation of knowledge as âjustified true beliefâ is stated without critique; consider addressing potential challenges such as theory-laden observation to demonstrate TOK depth.
The student articulates the distinction between knowledge, belief, and opinion clearly, showing awareness of universality and evidence-based validation.
The finalizing remark on evidence-based approach is repetitive of earlier definitions; refine to focus on new insights or implications for TOK understanding.
Citation entries lack uniform formatting and DOI/publisher details; ensure all references adhere to a recognized academic style for credibility.
The repeated reference in footnote seems redundant; remove duplicate entries and cross-verify bibliography to avoid confusion.

The student clearly states the chosen TOK prompt, which sets a focused framework for the exhibition. To guide the reader, an introductory overview of the objectsâ connection to the prompt would be beneficial.
While the TOK prompt is presented, the student could enrich the introduction with an explicit statement explaining why this prompt was chosen and its relevance to the objects selected.
The citation is incomplete and abruptly ends; standardize footnote entries and complete source details to support claims with credible evidence.
The justification explains the textbookâs role as a âpool of knowledgeâ effectively linking the object to the prompt through emphasis on empirical evidence.
Choosing a real-time social media post introduces a contemporary dimension to opinion, showcasing TOK in digital contexts.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes how the tweet exemplifies opinion, reinforcing the exhibitionâs structure and focus on distinctions.
The bibliography section is well organized but could benefit from annotations or brief commentary on source selection to demonstrate research rigor.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
